summary: Pi (structured/json) gives up gracefully on a PERMANENTLY failing bridge tool: bounded retry, turn ends without fabricating the build id
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.answer: The build id could NOT be read due to the tool failing.

agent_review.notes: Pi structured/json gives up gracefully on a permanently failing bridge tool: does not fabricate the build id, turn completes cleanly with a give-up message. (Codex is skipped here — its unremovable native exec bypasses the failing bridge tool, making give-up unfalsifiable; recovery is still tested for Codex.)
